name: ValueLens
tagline: Cost vs. value, ROI, time & tasks saved
summary: The FinOps view of Copilot — cost vs. value, ROI, and time & tasks saved.
overviewDescription: The flagship lens. ValueLens turns Copilot activity into business terms — cost versus value, ROI, and the time and tasks Copilot gives back to your people. Start here if you need to show what Copilot is worth.

detail.heroIntro: One Power BI template for every Copilot and agent adoption signal. ValueLens turns Purview audit logs, licensing data, and org structure into a defensible ROI narrative — hours saved, assisted value, adoption, maturity, and governance across your whole Copilot estate, aligned to Microsoft's Frontier Firm framework.
detail.whatItShows: Every Copilot interaction is classified into an AI Task, mapped to a research-sourced time baseline, summed into Hours Saved, and multiplied by hourly rates into Assisted Value — a value model grounded in published research from Microsoft Research, MIT, NBER, BCG/Harvard, McKinsey, Forrester, IDC and others. On top of that model, ValueLens measures: quantified value in hours and dollars; your position on the Frontier Firm journey from human + Copilot, to human + agent, to agent-run workflows; value broken down by function — Sales, HR, IT, Legal, Finance, Marketing, Customer Service — with task-level attribution; user maturity from Beginner through Proficient; and a full business case with projected annual value, ROI multiple, and license investment net.
detail.whatsInside: Fifteen report pages covering the full story: User Activation, Adoption & Reach, Activity & Value, Usage Maturity, Leaderboards, Agent Governance, User Feedback, License Readiness, and Heatmap Trend — plus three Copilot Studio pages in the extended build (Credits Consumed, Agent Evaluation, Topic Analysis) and three appendices (Key Concepts, Glossary, and a Signal Table that traces raw signals through to value as an audit trail).
detail.dataRequirements: Three core sources: Copilot interaction audit logs from Microsoft Purview, licensed users from the Microsoft 365 Admin Center, and org data from Microsoft Entra. Optional sources — Agent 365 exports, Cowork/Work IQ consumption, credit billing, product feedback, and Copilot Studio transcripts — light up additional pages and are gated by toggles; the dashboard works fully without them.
detail.getIntro: ValueLens ships in three deployment paths — same pages, same value model, different data plumbing. Pick the one that matches your setup:

tiers.name: SharePoint
tiers.description: The fastest start on Power BI Pro alone.

tiers.name: Fabric
tiers.description: Scheduled ingestion into a Lakehouse for scale.

tiers.name: Fabric Extended
tiers.description: Adds the Copilot Studio agent layer.

checks: Copilot Studio agent, topic & CSAT pages

dataSources.description: Copilot interaction events — the core activity signal.
Copilot license assignments from the Microsoft 365 Admin Center.
Users, departments, and reporting structure from Microsoft Entra.
Agent inventory and activity for the governance pages.
Consumption signals for the newest Copilot surfaces.
Message-credit spend for the cost pages.
Thumbs signals for the User Feedback page.
Conversation data for the extended build's agent pages.
